title: Week 4 HW — Crowdsourcing & Social Media Collaborations
html:
cs ethics essay on wikipedia as the canonical case of crowdsourcing and collaborative social media. Yahir argues both sides — democratization of knowledge vs. misinformation vulnerability — and lands on a Kantian read of contributor duty.

TL;DR

  • wikipedia framed as the working model of large-scale crowdsourcing — Wikipedians acting under transparent edit history.
  • Negative case: open-edit model creates misinformation windows that demand cross-referencing discipline.
  • kantian ethics read: contributors act under universalizable maxim of free knowledge as digital altruism.
  • oregon state cs ethics coursework — paired with the moral machine assignment as the term's two reflective writeups.

wikipedia · crowdsourcing · kantian ethics · cs ethics · oregon state
